paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments
summary: Problem 8 asks for the infimum of all constants C such that for every large enough n, the k-blow-up of every oriented tree of order n is (C^k · kn)-unavoidable. The paper establishes via Theorem 18 an upper bound of the form 2^(10+18k) · kn, which the authors acknowledge is far from tight; the maximum average degree of the k-blow-up of a tree of order n (equal to 2k − 2k/n) together with Proposition 2 gives a lower bound showing the optimal exponent is 2^Θ(k) · kn, but the precise constant C remains open. No follow-up paper resolving the exact infimum was found in the literature.
search_queries: blow-up oriented tree tournament unavoidable k-blow-up arXiv 2410.23566
k-blow-up oriented tree unavoidable tournament constant Aboulker Havet Lopes 2025

notes: No follow-up found. The problem is recent (October 2024). The paper itself establishes that the optimal unavoidability constant is of the form 2^Θ(k) · kn, narrowing the question to the base of the exponential, but the infimum of C in C^k · kn is not yet determined. Lucas Picasarri-Arrieta's 2025 publications do not include a follow-up on this specific problem.
